channel.title: KiddieDaily — News for Families
channel.description: Daily kid-friendly news with bias indicators and fact checks. Updated every morning.

item.title: 'World cup pubs boost' and 'Kate holds court'
item.description: Late openings of pubs and Catherine appearing at Wimbledon lead Friday's front pages. — 4 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: How the American Dream has survived 250 years, but only just
item.description: The American Dream has persisted since the days of the Founding Fathers, but faith in the ideal is fading — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Photos: Fans gather in the Bay Area for the World Cup match between the U.S. and Bosnia-Herzegovina
item.description: World Cup watch parties in Santa Clara and the San Francisco Bay Area brought fans together for the United States vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina match, where the U.S. earned a 2-0 win to reach the Round of 16 — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Rare copy of US Declaration of Independence found by volunteer in UK archives
item.description: The newly discovered copy is one of only 11 of its kind in the world. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Weekly quiz: Why did Catherine climb the UK's three highest peaks?
item.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days? — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
item.description: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has rep — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: New calculator reveals whether you should really worry about statin side effects
item.description: Scientists at the University of Oxford have created a calculator that predicts a person's individual risk of serious muscle disorders from statin medications. Their analysis found that more than 98% o — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists reveal what really happens when water is trapped in tiny spaces
item.description: A decades-old puzzle about water has finally been unraveled. Researchers found that water trapped in tiny nanoscale spaces is not inherently more reactive. Instead, the intense pressures created insid — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Great ape laughter reveals a hidden origin of human speech
item.description: The rhythm of human laughter appears to have deep evolutionary roots shared with chimpanzees, bonobos, gorillas, and orangutans. That ancient pattern may offer one of the clearest clues yet to how the — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Cruise ship docked in San Francisco hit by norovirus outbreak
item.description: A Princess Cruises cruise ship docked in San Francisco has seen an outbreak of norovirus, according to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The CDC said that 102 out of the over 3,000 — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Relief efforts shift as Venezuela quake survivors seek aid
item.description: Emergency relief is becoming the focus in Venezuela’s La Guaira state as aid stations distribute food. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists create quantum sound device that could transform communications
item.description: A new quantum device can generate precisely controlled bursts of sound-like particles, or phonons, by forcing electrons through an ultra-thin crystal at extremely low temperatures. The surprising beha — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Tiny magnetic waves could unlock quantum computers the size of a penny
item.description: A major breakthrough in quantum technology has turned magnons, tiny magnetic waves once considered too short-lived for practical use, into promising carriers of quantum information. Researchers extend — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: 390 gravitational wave detections reveal hidden population of black holes
item.description: Astronomers have released the largest gravitational wave catalog ever, revealing 161 new black hole collisions and pushing the total number of detections to 390. Among the highlights are the clearest — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Physicists Discover Superconducting States That Get Stronger Under Conditions Meant to Kill Them
item.description: Graphene keeps getting weirder... ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: White House to launch Trump Accounts on July 4
item.description: The Trump administration will launch new investment accounts for children on July 4, with the initiative intended to boost the financial standing of millions of Americans under the age of 18. Dubbed “ — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Have scientists really made a living cell from scratch? Not quite
item.description: A prototype cell partly capable of replicating itself has been created using 36 existing bacterial genes, but it's not really a living organism – yet — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Newly formed Tropical Storm Douglas poses no threat to land, hurricane center says
item.description: Douglas was located about 1,220 miles west-southwest of the southern tip of Mexico's Baja California, the National Hurricane Center said. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Don't expect trackers to save your stolen car, experts say
item.description: Kia told the BBC UK law prevented its location tracking function being used to live track vehicles. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Apple butter, sightseeing and puppets: How UK is marking US independence milestone
item.description: As America turns 250 years old, people across the pond are running events of their own to mark the big breakup. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.
